tasks.description: Compare WEM-style world/ego disentanglement, layered video generation, and object-centric generation; decide whether v1 needs explicit object/contact and robot state supervision.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Borrow WEM / HTEWorld-style long-horizon checks such as chunk-boundary dynamics, phase-matched motion, instruction-step retrieval, and phase-hop consistency, but add manipulation-specific checks for robot-action alignment, object/contact preservation, IDM recoverability, and reward-model usefulness.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Draft saved to ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/methods-comparison.md (182 lines, 8.3KB). Compares WEM world/ego vs layered video vs object-centric on 9 axes with a rating table; concludes no single family covers v1. Decision: v1 needs explicit object/contact and robot state supervision, but weak/partial signals suffice (synthetic bootstrap + pseudo-labels + robot proprioception). v1 method recipe combines layered-video scene machinery + object-centric slots with explicit pose and contact-mode head + WEM-style ego stream jointly trained with an IDM head.

comments.author: Host verification
comments.body: Host verification passed: inspected feibo remote artifact ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/methods-comparison.md (182 lines). It compares WEM-style world/ego, layered video, and object-centric routes and makes a concrete v1 decision: explicit object/contact + robot-state supervision with weak/partial labels. Accepted as methods decision deliverable.

tasks.title: Run Qwen-Image-Layered decomposition probe
tasks.description: Test Qwen/Qwen-Image-Layered on one poster-like image and one robot manipulation still; export generated RGBA layers plus a manifest; score recomposition error, duplicate or missing elements, layer purity, and whether scene/background, object/contact, and robot/ego regions are separable.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Qwen-Image-Layered is the first direct layer-generation probe because its model card describes input-image decomposition into multiple RGBA layers, variable layer counts, and recursive decomposition. Reference: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen-Image-Layered. Treat the output as editable visual layers first; only promote it to world-model evidence if QA shows meaningful scene/background, object/contact, and robot/ego separation.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Env probed. GPU: NVML driver mismatch (kernel 580.142 vs userspace 580.159.03) — cannot use CUDA without sudo to fix. Stack: built fresh venv via python3 -m venv + get-pip.py bootstrap; installed torch 2.12 CPU, diffusers 0.38, transformers 5.9, huggingface_hub 1.16. QwenImageLayeredPipeline import OK. Model is 57.7 GB across 33 files; HF reachable. Started snapshot_download in background, currently at ~4 GB. Hardware: AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D (16 threads), 61 GB RAM (58 GB free), no working GPU. bf16 weights (~58 GB) will fit RAM tightly. Plan: when download finishes, run probe with reduced steps (4-10) to validate pipeline; if CPU inference is infeasible in reasonable time, mark blocked with exact unblock action (fix NVIDIA driver to enable CUDA).

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Pipeline scaffold complete. Files under ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/: make_test_images.py (synthetic poster + robot_still 640x640 RGBA, fixed RNG), baseline_decompose.py (k-means RGBxy K=4 control), probe_qwen_image_layered.py (Qwen pipeline runner), score_layers.py (layer-qa-metrics.md metrics: recon L1, pairwise IoU, disjointness/coverage, layer purity, dup/missing proxy, scene/object/robot separability via synthetic GT regions), probe-report.md (draft report with §4 baseline numbers filled, §5 awaiting Qwen). Scoring pipeline verified on baseline: poster object_contact max-IoU 0.336, scene 0.324; robot_still object_contact max-IoU 0.036 (cup poorly isolated), robot_ego 0.160, scene 0.291 — baseline cannot isolate manipulation entities. Bar Qwen must clear is on object_contact and robot_ego separability. Model download still running (last check ~6 GB / 58 GB).

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Download progress: 6.3 GB / 58 GB after 4:42 (rate ~1.34 GB/min). 7 of 33 large shards still in-flight (transformer + text_encoder shards). Watcher b8m74w2e4/bw6qv73l6 armed to fire on 'DONE in' marker. Session waiting in shell for that notification — no work I can safely do in parallel without risk of cache contention.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Download complete: 35:42, 33/33 files, 54 GB cache. Snapshot dir ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/.hf-cache/models--Qwen--Qwen-Image-Layered/snapshots/8f0ca708dfff6ba1dd5f2d85d78f8c108a040bcf. Launching GPU probe with enable_model_cpu_offload (32 GB VRAM < 58 GB bf16 weights).

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Probe complete on GPU bf16 (RTX 5090) with sequential_cpu_offload. Each image: 50 steps, 6.18s/step, ~6:30 total. Both images decomposed into 4 RGBA layers. Artifacts at ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/outputs/{poster,robot_still}/{layer_0..3.png,manifest.json,score.json}. KEY RESULT — separability max-IoU vs k-means baseline: scene 0.92 vs 0.29 (~3x), object_contact poster 0.51 vs 0.34, robot_still 0.21 vs 0.036 (~6x), robot_ego robot_still 0.14 vs 0.16 (Qwen slightly worse — does not isolate the agent). Recomposition L1: 0.011-0.019 (over-composite soft alpha). Layers overlap by design (pairwise IoU up to 0.56, disjointness deficit 0.76); these are not failures but the over-composite structure. Conclusion: Qwen-Image-Layered is strong for the scene/background layer of our taxonomy, partial for object/contact, NOT useful for robot/ego — confirms methods-comparison.md §4 that v1 needs explicit object/contact + robot-state supervision on top. Full writeup at ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/probe-report.md (251 lines).

tasks.title: Prototype ChatGPT + Photoshop PSD layer pipeline
tasks.description: Use the white-background element-splitting prompt to generate one image per visual element without changing relative positions, then use Photoshop scripting/actions to remove white, keep every element as a separate layer, and export a PSD for inspection.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Use this as the practical Adobe Photoshop route: ask the image model to split every element into fixed-position white-background images with no duplicate elements, then assemble a PSD where white is removed and each element remains on its own layer. Reference workflow: https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1JxovBrEYW. Photoshop's own export flow can save individual layers as separate files, so this also gives a reverse check for whether the PSD has clean layer boundaries.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: PSD pipeline implemented and verified end-to-end on Linux. Files under ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/: build_psd.py (pytoshop assembly), psd_roundtrip.py (psd-tools extract + re-score), psd/{poster,robot_still}.psd (4 layers each, 7.8 MB), psd/{poster,robot_still}_manifest.json, outputs_psd_roundtrip/{poster,robot_still}/layer_{0..3}.png, outputs_psd_roundtrip/score_summary.json, psd-pipeline.md (writeup). VERIFIER RESULT: round-trip score diff vs original Qwen layers is 0.0000 on all metrics (recon_l1, separability max-IoU for scene/object_contact/robot_ego) — PSD round-trip is lossless. Run commands: '.venv/bin/python build_psd.py && .venv/bin/python psd_roundtrip.py'. Adaptations vs original spec: (1) substituted Qwen-Image-Layered RGBA outputs for ChatGPT white-background element images (no ChatGPT image-gen here); (2) used pytoshop for PSD writes instead of Photoshop scripting. The pipeline is drop-in ready for actual ChatGPT outputs once available on a Mac/Windows workstation — psd_roundtrip.py will read those PSDs without modification.

tasks.title: Define layer decomposition QA metrics
tasks.description: For each layered output, compute reconstruction / recomposition error, duplicate or missing elements, layer mask overlap, object/contact preservation, robot/ego isolation, and whether the layers support IDM / reward-model targets.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Do not score the first probes by visual cleanliness only. A useful layer set should recompose back to the input, avoid duplicate or missing elements, keep contact regions readable, isolate robot/ego pixels when present, and expose enough structure to train IDM, reward-model, or augmentation targets.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Draft + verifier saved under ~/workspace/image-layered-world-model/: layer-qa-metrics.md (340 lines, 11.4KB), verifier.py (7.1KB), verifier-run.log. Metric groups: §3 reconstruction/recomposition (composite + per-layer + cross-recomposition), §4 duplicate/missing/phantom slot rates, §5 pairwise mask overlap + disjointness/coverage deficits, §6 object/contact preservation (pose, contact recall/precision, penetration, sliding consistency), §7 robot/ego isolation (containment, leakage, FK self-consistency, embodiment-swap invariance), §8 IDM/reward-target support (IDM err vs pixel baseline, policy-imitation gap, reward AUROC, augmentation lift). Each metric defined with formula + units + threshold. §10 failure-mode coverage table maps every survey-notes/methods failure mode to at least one metric. Verifier exit=0; PASS on §3.1, §5.1, §5.2, §6.1, §6.2, §7.2, §8.1 stubs on a synthetic disjoint 4-frame example.

Artifacts layer-qa-metrics.md/verifier.py/verifier-run.log define and sanity-check reconstruction, duplicate/missing, mask overlap, object/contact, robot/ego, and IDM/reward support metrics.

tasks.title: Survey temporal-scaling skill representations
tasks.description: Compare DMP / arc-length DMP, action-chunk latent editing, and phase-variable policies for separating task geometry from playback speed; start from https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.03861.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Survey complete. Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_skill_representations.md (243 lines). Covers (1) goal of decoupling task geometry from playback speed, (2) four method families — vanilla DMP, arc-length / phase-decoupled DMP, action-chunk latent editing, phase-variable policies — with mechanism + temporal-scaling pros/cons each, (3) 9-axis comparison table weighted for rate-curve teleop needs, (4) v1 recommendation: two-layer composition (phase-decoupled trajectory + rate scheduler + TOPPRA/Ruckig retiming projection), (5) explicit decision (use phase-decoupled representation; action-chunk only re-enters if SFT-frequency task validates training-time speed conditioning), (6) source list including arXiv:2102.03861 (DMP tutorial survey), 1707.07239 (TOPPRA), 2105.04830 (Ruckig), (7) 3 open questions feeding sampling/event-constraint/SFT-frequency tasks. Decision: phase-decoupled representation for v1; arc-length DMP for analytic baselines + phase-variable policy where SFT already outputs over progress variable.

comments.author: Host verification
comments.body: Host verification passed: inspected feibo artifact ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_skill_representations.md (243 lines). It compares vanilla DMP, arc-length/phase-decoupled DMP, action-chunk latent editing, and phase-variable policies, gives a weighted comparison, and makes a concrete v1 decision: phase-decoupled trajectory + rate scheduler + TOPPRA/Ruckig projection. Accepted as survey deliverable.

tasks.title: Build classical retiming baselines
tasks.description: Test TOPPRA / Ruckig-style retiming under velocity, acceleration, jerk, torque, and contact constraints before adding learned timing; references: https://arxiv.org/abs/1707.07239 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.04830.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Retiming survey complete. Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_retiming.md (215 lines). Key framing: TOPPRA and Ruckig are not competitors but different layers — TOPPRA is offline path parameterization (consumes a path + global limits, emits time-optimal velocity profile under velocity/accel/torque/contact), Ruckig is online per-tick OTG (consumes current+target state, emits jerk-limited transition). Constraint coverage table shows TOPPRA owns torque+contact offline, Ruckig owns jerk+real-time. v1 decision: adopt BOTH — TOPPRA for offline retiming-projection of rate-scheduler intent, Ruckig for online jerk-limited transitions on event-predicate changes. Learned timing deferred until classical baseline is measured (verifier plan §7 specifies pass/fail thresholds for feasibility, jerk ratio, extra time cost). Sources: arXiv:1707.07239 (TOPPRA), arXiv:2105.04830 (Ruckig).

comments.author: Host verification
comments.body: Host verification passed: inspected feibo artifact ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_retiming.md (215 lines). It correctly separates TOPPRA as offline path parameterization and Ruckig as online jerk-limited trajectory generation, covers velocity/acceleration/jerk/torque/contact constraints, and defines concrete verifier thresholds. Accepted as classical retiming baseline deliverable.

tasks.title: Design phase-aware sampling strategies
tasks.description: Sampling is still unclear; compare intuitive templates such as fast in free space, slow near contact, hold during perception / settling, and re-accelerate after stable force / tactile contact.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Sampling strategies design complete. Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_sampling_strategies.md (208 lines). 6 templates with concrete predicates + failure modes + recovery: T0 constant baseline, T1 sharp two-rate (free-space vs near-contact with hysteresis), T2 distance-graded slowdown, T3 hold during perception/settling/grasp-unconfirmed (composes onto T1/T2), T4 ramped re-acceleration gated on (force_stable AND tactile_stable for tau_stable) with debounce, T5 learned residual on top of classical (clipped to safety floor). Comparison table across 7 axes. v1 composition: T3∘T4∘T1 (sharp two-rate base + perception-hold overlay + gated ramp re-entry); switch to T2 once d_contact estimator is reliable; T5 behind feature flag until SFT/RL story (task_rate_curve_objective_split + task_rate_curve_rl) lands. Verifier plan §7 with concrete pass criteria (T0 must lose on contact, T2 within +10% time vs T1 on free-space, T3 zero completion failures added, T4 zero force overshoot, T5 ≥10% time improvement with no safety regression). 4 open questions feeding event_constraints + SFT-frequency + RL tasks.

tasks.title: Mark event constraints in teleop demos
tasks.description: Do not accelerate perception waits, grasp confirmation, contact settling, or object state transitions blindly.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Event constraints inventory complete. Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_event_constraints.md (195 lines). 4 events fully operationalized — E1 perception wait (predicate: awaiting reply OR stale beyond τ_perception_stale; hold hard), E2 grasp confirmation (force ≥ f_grasp_min ∧ tactile area ≥ A_grasp_min ∧ optional vision OK for τ_grasp_stable; hold hard; timeout to retry then needs_user after N=3), E3 contact settling (force+tactile rolling-window variance below threshold for τ_stable; soft slowdown, then T4 ramp resumes), E4 object state transition (event labeler emits transition_in_progress/complete/aborted; hold hard; aborts escalate). Composition: hold = OR over all four; ds/dt = 0 if hold else T1/T2/T4 result; hysteretic, with timeouts that escalate not auto-clear. Detection plumbing classified tier-A (deployable v1: E1 E2 E3 on Franka/Cobot-Magic/Piper-X/Linker-FFG) vs tier-B (E4 behind feature flag until FSM or learned labeler ships). Verifier plan §7 with adversarial sensor-dropout tests. Key safety invariant: speed reward only computed when hold=False, so RL cannot learn to short-circuit predicates. 4 open questions on per-embodiment threshold calibration + labeler choice + clipping integration + per-task overrides.

tasks.title: Define DAgger / SFT / RL training split
tasks.description: DAgger collects correction data and SFT improves stability / safety; RL starts from that policy and adds speed rewards from velocity / completion time plus smoothness rewards from acceleration and jerk.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_objective_split.md (190 lines). Three-stage pipeline: DAgger (correction data; triggered by event predicates so corrections are captured at the right moments; ends when correction_rate < 0.05 for 20 rollouts), SFT (BC over phase-decoupled action chunks with hold-aware masking so model doesn't learn to drive through perception waits/grasp/settling/object transitions), RL (KL-anchored fine-tune on top with reward = w_speed·R_speed + w_smooth·R_smooth + w_safety·R_safety + w_KL·R_KL). KEY INVARIANT (reward gating table): R_speed is gated on hold=False — RL cannot earn speed reward by short-circuiting event predicates. KEY INVARIANT (handoff): DAgger never sees speed reward; SFT loss is hold-aware; RL starts from π_SFT not scratch; event predicates read by all stages; Ruckig is in the action path during RL rollouts so jerk-limit violations are infeasible by construction. 4 risks documented (DAgger plateau, SFT pace overfit, reward gating bypass via sensor dropout, action-chunk RL interface). Pass criteria per stage in §7; if a stage fails, the next does not start.

tasks.title: Ablate SFT action sampling frequency
tasks.description: Train SFT variants with different action sampling frequencies / downsampling ratios, then compare execution speed, action continuity, jerk, and success stability before RL.

comments.author: Artifact
comments.body: Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_sft_action_frequency.md (186 lines). DESIGN-ONLY — no fake numbers, no runs yet. Ablation grid: 8 SFT variants = 4 sampling rates {5, 20, 50, 100} Hz × 2 chunking modes {single-action, K=16 chunk}, all trained from same D_dagger ∪ D_teleop with identical optimizer/seeds (3 seeds final). 8 metrics covering speed (T_complete), safety (success_rate, hold_violation_rate, jerk_ratio_to_limit), smoothness (continuity_L2, jerk_peak measured both pre-Ruckig and post-Ruckig, over_correction_count), and deployability (compute_latency_ms < 1000/f_act). PASS CRITERIA: variant must clear jerk_ratio<1, hold_violation_rate=0, latency budget, success ≥ baseline-0.05. Among passers Pareto-rank on (T_complete, jerk_peak, continuity_L2) and pick closest to human-demo time stats (SFT not allowed to outpace human — that's RL's job). Evaluation protocol mandates split by demo-identity not frame, simulator first with same Ruckig layer as production, real-robot validation on top-2 only. 3 named risks (downsampling artefact, chunk-vs-single confounding, baseline_human_success definition) with mitigations. 3 open questions on N_eval sizing, frequency-conditioned variant, real-robot wear budget.

tasks.description: Use force / tactile signals to detect unsafe or unstable rollouts and request correction, train the SFT base, then RL-tune only smoother and faster execution; compare operation types to see which benefit from acceleration.
tasks.result: Archived as inactive Survey TODO on 2026-06-12. This remains background/scoping material, but it is no longer an active dashboard TODO.

comments.author: Artifact
comments.body: Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_rl.md (260 lines). PLAN ONLY — explicit non-claims in §12: no model trained, no DAgger run, no empirical numbers. Pilot scope: 3 op types spanning acceleration-benefit spectrum (free-space reach, pick-place with contact, precision insertion). Safety detector with 5 rules R1..R5 (force threshold, tactile area Δ, slip via tactile-vs-path-tangent, contact-mode inconsistency vs E3 predicate, impedance residual). DAgger termination: correction_rate<0.05 over 20 rollouts AND zero un-corrected safety incidents. SFT stage gates on the criteria from objective_split §7. RL: KL-regularized policy gradient with reward weights w_speed=1.0, w_smooth=0.3, w_safety=10.0, w_KL=0.1; 2-axis sweep on (w_smooth, w_KL). Per-op-type targets: free-space ≥30% T_complete + ≥20% jerk reduction; pick-place ≥15% T_complete + ≥20% jerk; insertion 0% speed (bounded by perception) + ≥30% jerk. Headline output (§6) is op-type benefit comparison TABLE — TBD cells make explicit which numbers are not measured. Verifier §7 specifies CSV/JSON logs + validity criteria (no untrapped safety incidents, KL bound holding, termination reason recorded). 5 risks documented including DAgger expense on hardware, safety-detector false positives, op-type generalization.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: New project framing: human demo data should be treated as path, phase, contact-event, and safety priors, not as the direct source of superhuman execution. The superhuman part likely requires RL finetune on top of SFT / retiming, with rewards for completion time, velocity profile, recovery behavior, smoothness, and strict force / tactile / event guardrails. Hardware is part of the claim: Wuji should be paired with a robot arm whose servo bandwidth, velocity, acceleration, and latency can approach human real-time execution; Flexiv / 非夕-style arms are a candidate to evaluate before claiming speed beyond replay.

The next model-load issue was DiffSynth's default converted-safetensors redirect for T5/VAE, so the remote train entrypoint was patched to call `WanVideoPipeline.from_pretrained(..., redirect_common_files=False)` and the run script now points `--tokenizer_path` at the local Wan2.2 tokenizer. The slow converted-checkpoint download was stopped. Current remote state: tmux `wan22_train_wait` is active and waiting for a free L40S GPU (`<2GB` used and `<10%` util) before launching the patched LoRA run. tasks.title: 用 step-125 做 held-out GT / base WAN / LoRA 对比 gallery
tasks.description: Run post-train quality evaluation for the existing Wan2.2-TI2V-5B LoRA step-125 checkpoint on the UMI binding machine. Use held-out clips, compare base WAN, step-125 LoRA, and GT side by side, and keep prompt/caption, first-frame, left/right concat metadata, run_id, model path, and metrics. Acceptance: at least 16 held-out examples for fast inspection, preferably 50 for dashboard/paper gallery; record output paths and a short quality conclusion.

comments.author: Boris / Codex

tasks.title: 补 object/contact supervision probe
tasks.description: Why now: object_contact 在 robot_still 只有约 0.21，说明单靠 layered image 不足。Deliverable: object/contact head 的弱监督方案，对比 color/geometry/Qwen layer features。Acceptance: poster 和 robot_still 都输出 object/contact mask；记录 contact recall/precision、penetration/sliding proxy。

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [2026-05-23] Probe v0 finished. Built 5 heads (qwen_baseline / color_saliency / geom_compact / qwen_filtered / color_x_qwen). Run: .venv/bin/python contact_object_head.py. Robot_still cup IoU: H2 geom_compact 0.810 vs Qwen 0.212 (3.8x lift), precision 1.0, penetration_vs_gt_scene 0.0. Poster silhouette IoU: H2 geom_compact 0.856 vs Qwen 0.514 (1.7x lift), precision 1.0, penetration_vs_gt_scene 0.0. Contact event proxy (touch-within-4px-of-robot): TP=1 FP=0 FN=0 TN=1 across both scenes for all heads — sanity, not discriminating, N=2. Sliding proxy: unmeasurable_single_image (defer to temporal probe). Key reads: (1) color is the strongest feature; (2) Qwen non-base layers alone are worse than nothing (H3 IoU 0.016/0.242, penetration 0.95/0.75 — floods the image); (3) Qwen layers do not add info on top of color (H4 ≈ H2); (4) chained head: robot mask subtraction is essential. Penetration vs Qwen base = 1.0 for every head (uninformative; Qwen base alpha=1 everywhere); replaced with penetration_vs_gt_scene as primary. Artifacts: contact_object_head.py, contact-object-head.md, outputs_contact_object_head/score_summary.json, outputs_contact_object_head/{robot_still,poster}/mask_*.png + overlays. Acceptance: poster+robot_still both produce object/contact mask; contact recall/precision recorded; penetration proxy recorded; sliding marked unmeasurable as expected.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [2026-05-23] Probe v0 finished. Built explicit robot/ego head 'chromatic_dark_cc' = (chroma<0.08 AND lum<0.55) -> 4-CC -> keep CCs touching URDF-projected base box. Run: .venv/bin/python robot_ego_head.py. Results vs synthetic GT (outputs_robot_ego_head/score_summary.json): robot_still robot_ego IoU 0.625 (precision 0.988, recall 0.629) vs Qwen baseline 0.138 — 4.5x lift. Poster false-positive control: 0 px (vs chromatic_dark alone which lights up the dark title bar at 31k px). The URDF base-box CC step is the load-bearing piece that suppresses no-robot false positives. Recall ceiling 0.629 is a GT-bbox artifact (slanted arm polygon inside an axis-aligned rect); precision 0.988 confirms head is tight. Report: robot-ego-head.md. Overlays: outputs_robot_ego_head/robot_still/overlay_{qwen,cd_cc}.png. Acceptance met (robot_still robot_ego max-IoU >> Qwen baseline).

tasks.title: 把单图 layer probe 扩展到短视频 temporal consistency
tasks.description: Why now: world model 要服务视频/IDM，PSD/单图 round-trip 只是静态验证。Deliverable: 3-5 帧短序列 layer propagation 或逐帧 decomposition + consistency metric。Acceptance: 输出 temporal flicker / mask IoU over time / object-contact continuity 指标，并列出最小训练目标。

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [2026-05-23] Probe v0 finished. 5-frame synthetic sequence: arm reaches cup at t=2, slides it +8/+18 px at t=3,4. Per-frame heads = robot_ego (chromatic_dark_cc with frame-adaptive base box) + object_contact (geom_compact with fixed workspace prior, not per-frame EE box — key v1 change so cup is detected pre-contact). GT geometry promoted from axis-aligned bbox to polygon-level (matches drawn shapes), lifting IoU ceiling. Run: .venv/bin/python make_video_frames.py && .venv/bin/python video_temporal_probe.py. Headline metrics: per-frame IoU >=0.97 across S/O/R for all 5 frames. Temporal pair: |pred_iou - gt_iou| < 0.015 on every pair (head change-rate tracks true motion-rate); flicker rate stays at the 'real geometric motion' floor (scene <0.6%, object 0% pre-contact / 33-41% sliding, robot 4-16% depending on tip-speed). Contact timeline: pred==GT exactly (F,F,T,T,T), TP=3 FP=0 FN=0 TN=2, recall=1.0 precision=1.0, 1 transition both sides, no flicker transitions. Sliding consistency mean_cos = 0.9996 across 2 contact pairs. Centroid drift per layer cleanly separates static scene (<0.2 px/frame jitter) vs sliding cup (~9 px/frame during contact) vs reaching arm (~7.7 px/frame reach -> 1.9 px/frame contact). Minimum training objectives listed in §9 of video-temporal-probe.md: (1) per-frame recon, (2) layer-isolated recon, (3) static-layer temporal-IoU, (4) motion-warped IoU for moving layers, (5) contact-event continuity (dwell threshold), (6) slot-identity Hungarian, (7) sliding-direction cosine, (8) kinematic-feasibility on robot centroid. Min set for v1: 1+3(scene)+4(warp)+5. Artifacts: make_video_frames.py, video_temporal_probe.py, video-temporal-probe.md, outputs_video_temporal_probe/score_summary.json + per-frame masks + overlays, test_video/.

tasks.title: 实现 drag/teleop/mixed fine-tuning loader baseline
tasks.description: Why now: comparison TODO 需要先有统一 loader，才谈训练结果。Deliverable: loader + split config，支持 teleop-only、drag-only、70/30 mixed 三路。Acceptance: dry-run 打印 batch shapes、episode counts、missing stream report。

comments.author: Result
comments.body:
Baseline loader delivered + dry-run + tests.

ARTIFACTS (sha256)
- /home/lv-robotics/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/training/drag_demo_loader.py = cc5588dbd91b966218442374c5463187d08b02ae446abfff368a8fb0f9b7ef9c
- /home/lv-robotics/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/training/dry_run_loader.py   = d9955093bcb434e9eae89fb04f865d9e0d7536450fac6db850bfb5c0d9799805
- /home/lv-robotics/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/training/test_drag_demo_loader.py = fdeaac339d334477d7f6086b2db2b65fedbb651134c0e5080278367313932500
- /home/lv-robotics/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/training/split_config.json   = 180a80b2f4935d4442beb2b6d7b885d3327fb1f42d287429a06d265714fc9000
- /home/lv-robotics/workspace/.verifier_reports/loader_dry_run.json        = 4eb0498b6a1dcd43001d6d700d5c630c00e9efd3c6afa0dbaca3422f832aea45 (dry-run report)

DESIGN
- Dataset class DragDemoDataset (PyTorch); each item is one timestep: rgb dict per cam (uint8 HxWx3), state float32 (14,), action float32 (14,) sampled +100 ms forward in state.pkl time; meta {source, dataset, episode, episode_idx, t_sample_ms}.
- Sampling grid: state.pkl (~60 Hz). For each state index we nearest-lookup the RGB frame on every camera via image_timestamps.pkl — handles per-camera asynchronous rates without forcing global sync.
- split_config.json schema (drag_demo_split.v1): sources = {drag, teleop}, each pointing at a manifest path + episode_filter; modes = {drag_only, teleop_only, mixed_70_30}. Today teleop.manifest=null because no teleop has been collected yet.
- Mixture rule: anchor universe on the binding source. If a mode requires share>0 from a source with 0 loaded episodes, the dataset returns 0 episodes and tags 'mixture_degraded' in sources_summary — refuses to silently downgrade a 70/30 mix to drag-only when teleop is missing.

DRY-RUN (cmd: cd ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/training && ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python dry_run_loader.py --batch-size 4 --samples-per-episode 4 --image-size 320 180 --json-out ~/workspace/.verifier_reports/loader_dry_run.json)

- mode=drag_only:    mixture {drag:1.0, teleop:0.0}; episodes=19 (manifest 19 -> loaded 19 -> after_mixture 19); samples=76; missing_streams=none.
  batch shapes (B=4): state (4,14) float32 ; action (4,14) float32 ; rgb[cam_top] (4,180,320,3) uint8 ; rgb[cam_left_wrist] (4,180,320,3) uint8 ; rgb[cam_right_wrist] (4,180,320,3) uint8 ; first meta: {source:'drag', dataset:'egg_to_bowl', episode:'0010', episode_idx:0, t_sample_ms:1779375420520}
- mode=teleop_only:  mixture {drag:0.0, teleop:1.0}; episodes=0; samples=0; missing_streams=[{source:'teleop', missing:'manifest', manifest_path:null}]; batch skipped (empty).
- mode=mixed_70_30:  mixture {drag:0.7, teleop:0.3}; episodes=0 (drag truncated to 0 because teleop has 0); samples=0; missing_streams=[{source:'teleop', missing:'manifest', manifest_path:null}]; batch skipped.

TESTS (cmd: cd ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/training && ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python -m unittest test_drag_demo_loader -v)
- Ran 10 tests in 1.496s OK ; covers: split_config parse + sums-to-1 ; unknown mode rejected ; drag_only yields >0 eps with no missing streams ; teleop_only with missing manifest yields 0 ; mixed refuses silent degrade with current data ; batch tensor shapes/dtypes on the real manifest ; synthetic-teleop manifest test where mixed_70_30 actually mixes ~70/30 with both sources tagged in meta.

HOW TO ENABLE MIXED ONCE TELEOP DATA EXISTS
1. Run sop_episode_verifier.py on the teleop raw_data root to produce a teleop verifier JSON.
2. Run build_initial_batch_manifest.py with --out /…/teleop_batch_manifest.json on that JSON.
3. Edit split_config.json -> sources.teleop.manifest to that path.
4. Rerun dry_run_loader.py; mixed_70_30 will report non-zero episode counts and real batch shapes.

REMAINING REMOTELY-UNFINISHABLE WORK
- Actual teleop data collection (separate physical session) is not something a remote Claude session can perform.
- Sequence augmentation (action chunks / image stacking) is downstream of this baseline; pick after first training pass.

comments.author: Result
comments.body:
Initial-batch manifest refreshed against accepted 0.10 rad threshold; loader contract verified end-to-end.

ARTIFACTS (sha256)
- build_initial_batch_manifest.py (writer): f34131f85235a367326b8680fc353ac615a7e674b730e81a85aae49280135236  → /home/lv-robotics/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection/build_initial_batch_manifest.py
- load_initial_batch.py (loader smoke test): abcb4659dbf1cd92909852bdead0907e828a185e03f42d4424bb66c621d15c55  → /home/lv-robotics/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection/load_initial_batch.py
- initial_batch_manifest.json: 58b3ffb76eacfd1ba12cbd41796da82d9311d421d0001f7c3c487251d23d4ed1  → /home/lv-robotics/workspace/.verifier_reports/initial_batch_manifest.json (216 KB, schema_version drag_demo_initial_batch.v1)
- sop_episode_verifier.py (0.10 rad default): 0507afe8473f3c50df246a0799f6f8c2f866a8a93ffcfc9f99133371143cc7ff
- raw_data_full_report.json: 200c2b7669d05e123955f45623e2e9218d8b3465ffba450e8efd3f10dff487b2

MANIFEST CONTENTS
- demo_target: 100
- stillness_threshold_rad_used: 0.10
- eval_tier_strict_threshold_rad: 0.02
- n_counted_toward_target: 19
- delta_to_target: 81
- n_eval_tier_strict: 13  (kept as separate tier for terminal-stability scoring of policies)
- n_near_miss_stillness_only: 102 (file/stream/pkl all OK; only stillness fails)
- n_file_or_stream_fail: 11
- by_dataset_counted: { egg_scoop_v1: 3, egg_to_bowl: 16 }  (egg_scoop_v2 contributes 0/2 — 2-cam legacy)

LOADER VERIFICATION (real cv2/h5py/pickle opens, not just file existence)
- cmd: ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection/load_initial_batch.py --manifest ~/workspace/.verifier_reports/initial_batch_manifest.json --include-eval
- result: OK: all 19 counted episodes pass the loader contract (and 13 eval episodes) — exit=0
- verifier unit tests: Ran 13 tests in 1.137s OK exit=0

REPRODUCE (auto pipeline)
1. ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection/sop_episode_verifier.py --root ~/workspace/raw_data --report ~/workspace/.verifier_reports/raw_data_full_report.json
2. ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection/build_initial_batch_manifest.py --report ~/workspace/.verifier_reports/raw_data_full_report.json --out ~/workspace/.verifier_reports/initial_batch_manifest.json
3. ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection/load_initial_batch.py --manifest ~/workspace/.verifier_reports/initial_batch_manifest.json --include-eval
   → After each new onsite batch lands under ~/workspace/raw_data, rerun steps 1-3; manifest stays current automatically.

ONSITE COLLECTION GAP (the 81 episodes I cannot complete remotely)
- Stillness gate the operator must hit: drop hands off controls and count to 2 (per-joint motion <= 0.10 rad over the final 2 s on BOTH arms).
- Folder convention: <dataset>/000x for success; <dataset>/_failed/f000x for retained failures.
- 9 required files per episode: cam_top_rgb.mp4, cam_top_depth.h5, cam_left_wrist_rgb.mp4, cam_left_wrist_depth.h5, cam_right_wrist_rgb.mp4, cam_right_wrist_depth.h5, eef_pose.pkl, image_timestamps.pkl, state.pkl.
- Diversity: morning/afternoon/cloudy; green long gloves with sleeves; clean table (only pan + white plate); cam_top unoccluded.
- Operator distribution (from existing plan): Boris/Gaochen/Jingxiang each ~30 sequences; Xiaobin observes/participates.
- egg_scoop_v2 (2-cam legacy) is OUT of the candidate pool — re-collect under the 3-cam stack if its scenarios are still needed.

DOWNSTREAM HANDOFF

tasks.title: 决定 initial batch final-stillness threshold
tasks.description: Why now: 当前严格 0.02 rad 只有 13/100，relax 到 0.50 可到 94；不先定 threshold，后续 collect/fine-tune 都会反复。Deliverable: 选择 strict onsite recollect 或 relaxed threshold，并写入 verifier config。Acceptance: threshold、理由、风险和数据数量都记录在 TODO comment 中。

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [Codex route 2026-05-23] Route: do not wait on pure preference. Compute strict vs relaxed final-stillness thresholds on the current 13/100 curated batch, report how many episodes pass each, and recommend one threshold before scaling to 100. Masquerade motivates data editing, but this project’s pass/fail gate is contact/object preservation and downstream loader usefulness.

comments.author: Result
comments.body:
Decision: stillness_max_range_rad = 0.10 rad (≈5.7 deg) over a 2.0 s trailing window on both arms.

RATIONALE
- 0.02 rad (~1.15 deg) is below the arm-at-rest encoder/torque-balance noise plus passive gripper-finger relaxation after release. Yields 13/163 — production cannot meet it without retraining operators.
- 0.10 rad (~5.7 deg) keeps the 'operator actively halted the arm' signal: a wrist twitch or finger settle passes, but a still-rotating spatula does not. Yields 19/163 — modest gain, quality preserved.
- 0.20 rad (~11 deg) lets the right wrist still be actively moving at the marker, corrupting the task-end signal the policy learns. Reject.
- 0.50 rad (~29 deg) is no-op; rejected.

DATA COUNTS (raw_data, 163 episodes scanned)
- Before (0.02 rad): n_counted_toward_target = 13/100; n_near_miss_stillness_only = 108
- After  (0.10 rad): n_counted_toward_target = 19/100; n_near_miss_stillness_only = 102
  by_dataset: egg_to_bowl 16/103, egg_scoop_v1 3/27, egg_scoop_v2 0/2 (always missing cam_top)
  delta_to_target = 81

NEW BATCH PATHS (19; right-arm trailing range shown — all <= 0.10 rad):
  egg_scoop_v1/{0000,0004,0010}  (R 0.0023-0.0027)
  egg_to_bowl/{0000,0001,0003,0004,0006,0008,0009,0010,0015,0041,0054,0072,0093,0095,0096,0101}  (R 0.0027-0.0839)

RISK & MITIGATIONS
- Risk 1: Six newly-included episodes have right-arm range 0.04-0.09 rad (≈2-5 deg) — the wrist is settling, not perfectly halted. Impact on imitation-learning: policy may learn a slight 'residual jitter at task end' rather than a hard stop. Mitigation: keep a strict-tier eval set (the 13 episodes at <= 0.02 rad) for held-out scoring of the policy's terminal stability.
- Risk 2: Operators may notice the relaxation and trend looser over time. Mitigation: brief at the 2026-05-23 onsite session — 'after rotation, drop hands away from controls and pause to a full count of 2'. Keep the strict tier visible in the manifest so quality drift is detectable in subsequent verifier reports.
- Risk 3: egg_scoop_v2 still 0/2 because the 2-cam variant lacks cam_top. Not a threshold problem — drop egg_scoop_v2 from the candidate pool until it is re-collected with the 3-cam stack.

WRITTEN TO VERIFIER CONFIG
- VerifierConfig.stillness_max_range_rad default: 0.02 → 0.10 (sop_episode_verifier.py)
- CLI default for --stillness-threshold-rad: 0.02 → 0.10
- Tests still pass: cd ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection && ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python -m unittest test_sop_episode_verifier  →  Ran 13 tests OK exit=0

ARTIFACTS (sha256)
- sop_episode_verifier.py: 0507afe8473f3c50df246a0799f6f8c2f866a8a93ffcfc9f99133371143cc7ff
- raw_data_full_report.json (re-run at 0.10 rad): 200c2b7669d05e123955f45623e2e9218d8b3465ffba450e8efd3f10dff487b2
- initial_batch_manifest.json (re-built at 0.10 rad): d2f9c10938de6d521b2a814f3cf0827679688059e9432671c0f1b5c888ee53c7

REPRODUCE
- ~/miniconda3/envs/robo_ctrl/bin/python ~/workspace/ARX_A5_Dual_ARM/data_collection/sop_episode_verifier.py --root ~/workspace/raw_data --report ~/workspace/.verifier_reports/raw_data_full_report.json
- For strict tier (eval set): add --stillness-threshold-rad 0.02

DOWNSTREAM
- task_drag_demo_initial_batch stays needs_user: 19/100 known-good now, still need 81 physical demos (onsite work, not remotely reproducible).
- task_drag_demo_reach_100_manifest can proceed: manifest format is finalized; operators only need to feed new collections through the verifier and append new entries.

comments.author: Result
comments.body:
Schema v1 + writer + reader + validator + 10s fake-episode E2E shipped.

== Repo + commit ==

Worktree: /home/boris/workspace/real_robot_record
Branch:   master
Commit:   dea26fdbe8be321283bdfea48401d9b91474ac06  (Initial real_robot_record schema v1: writer, reader, validator, CLI, fake-episode E2E)

== Acceptance criteria (from task description) ==

  Deliverable      | Status | Where
  ------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------
  schema           | DONE   | src/real_robot_record/schema.py + docs/schema.md
  sample writer    | DONE   | src/real_robot_record/writer.py (EpisodeWriter)
  hand joints      | DONE   | kind=hand_joints (Wuji) + hand_skeleton (MANUS)
  tactile          | DONE   | kind=tactile / tactile_zones
  wrist pose       | DONE   | kind=wrist_pose (VIVE), body_pose (chest/arm)
  camera extrinsics| DONE   | kind=camera_extrinsics (4x4 snapshot or stream)
  robot state      | DONE   | kind=robot_state (q, qdot, ee_pose, optional wrench)
  time sync        | DONE   | ts_us in every stream (host UTC μs), 50 ms skew bound
  fake episode     | DONE   | rrr-write-fake CLI (10 s, 7 streams)
  validator        | DONE   | rrr-validate CLI + validate_episode() library
  JSON/HDF5        | DONE   | manifest.json + data.h5 per episode

== Verifier 1: pytest (offline, no hardware) ==

Command:
  cd ~/workspace/real_robot_record && ~/workspace/wuji_wrapper/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v

Result: 23 passed in 0.20 s
Coverage:
  - writer + reader round-trip (minimal + 7-stream full episode)
  - writer-side validation: unknown stream id / wrong shape / wrong payload
    field / missing payload / duplicate device / dangling stream / bad dtype /
    field name collides with index column / double-underscore id rejection
  - validator error paths: missing manifest, schema_version mismatch,
    sha256 mismatch after h5 edit, non-monotonic ts_us, n_frames mismatch,
    unknown stream kind (warn + strict-raise), missing payload dataset,
    non-4x4 calibration

Log:
  ~/.claude/jobs/real_robot_record/tests.log   sha256=8cdff44d52cfd368d56e4182e869072acd80e0f5bd5c76bd07a97eaa0056506c

== Verifier 2: 10s fake-episode E2E ==

Commands:
  rm -rf ~/.claude/jobs/real_robot_record/ep_fake_v2
  ~/workspace/wuji_wrapper/.venv/bin/rrr-write-fake --out ~/.claude/jobs/real_robot_record/ep_fake_v2 --duration 10
  ~/workspace/wuji_wrapper/.venv/bin/rrr-validate ~/.claude/jobs/real_robot_record/ep_fake_v2

Artifact:
  /home/boris/.claude/jobs/real_robot_record/ep_fake_v2/
    manifest.json   6184 bytes   sha256=f307d5580bdb8961a5c0601b95e2f9aafa965df636280bf28d74ab0dec9034ea
    data.h5         3921004 bytes sha256=4cc088461bc248a98049098f23dd7e6b7ec1b618384d315614b1c295c8d84f4d

HDF5 inventory (verified by h5py inspection):
  /streams/wuji_glove_right__tactile          ts_us(1200) seq(1200) frame_id(1200) data(1200,768)float32
  /streams/wuji_glove_right__hand_joint_angles ts_us(1200) seq(1200) frame_id(1200) joint_angles(1200,21)float32
  /streams/wuji_glove_right__imu_palm          ts_us(1200) seq(1200) frame_id(1200) orientation(1200,4) angular_velocity(1200,3) acceleration(1200,3) all float32
  /streams/manus_right__hand_skeleton          ts_us(1200) seq(1200) frame_id(1200) keypoints(1200,21,3)float32
  /streams/vive_right_wrist__pose              ts_us(1200) seq(1200) frame_id(1200) pose(1200,4,4)float32
  /streams/franka_left__joint_state            ts_us(500)  seq(500)  frame_id(500)  q(500,7) qdot(500,7) ee_pose(500,4,4) all float64
  /streams/camera_color_0__extrinsics          ts_us(1) seq(1) frame_id(1) T_world_cam(1,4,4)float64

Validator output:
  [rrr-validate] OK /home/boris/.claude/jobs/real_robot_record/ep_fake_v2

Verifier logs (all in ~/.claude/jobs/real_robot_record/):
  write_fake.log  sha256=821f0e1bae575e095b0d02e907b95a2eb59331e41426a7d8c011ebd8da6ac496
  validate.log    sha256=e2670d64a0c2000ccb2d6996453b6f632a0d76096053ba8ac902a3c76948242d

== Schema highlights ==

- SCHEMA_VERSION = 'real_robot_record.v1' (validator hard-rejects mismatches).
- All ts_us are host wall-clock UTC μs = int(time.time()*1e6) on the recorder.
  Cross-stream alignment bound (recommended): 50 ms.
- canonical_stream_id(device, name) = f"{device}__{name}" — no '__' allowed in
  either component; reserved field names ts_us/seq/frame_id cannot be reused
  as payload field names.
- Calibration matrices live in manifest only (small, human-editable). Stream
  payloads stay in HDF5 (binary, compressed).
- KNOWN_STREAM_KINDS is a closed enum for warnings; unknown kinds are
  warnings-by-default so the schema extends forward without a bump.

Status -> review. Companion repos using this schema:
  /home/boris/workspace/vive_wrapper       (MANUS+VIVE fusion -> FusedHandFrame -> can be appended to wrist_pose / hand_skeleton streams)
  /home/boris/workspace/wuji_glove_wrapper (FrameSample collected by net-smoke -> can be appended to tactile / imu / hand_joints streams)
  /home/boris/workspace/manus_wrapper      (GloveFrame.to_mediapipe() -> hand_skeleton stream)

Suggested follow-ups (separate TODOs):
  1. End-to-end wiring: an orchestrator that scans connected devices, calls
     the right wrapper per device kind, and feeds each frame into EpisodeWriter.
     Blocked on hardware (Wuji glove link, MANUS glove pairing, VIVE/SteamVR).
  2. Foxglove / mcap export for visualisation (h5 -> mcap is a tight loop).
  3. Validator profile flags for retargeting (e.g. require at least one
     wrist_pose stream when a hand_skeleton stream is present).

All 5 PASS validate_contact_pack.py (which chains validate_hoi_schema.py then layers patch SE(3) / +z=normal / area / friction / grasp_mode_v2 / tool_use / patch_id-link consistency / trajectory summary). Each sample carries the fields the Isaac verifier needs: object endpoint pose, per-patch SE(3) frames, link_name, friction, tool_tip targets. Grasp v2 taxonomy in spec §5 (9 values, Cutkosky-derived). Open issues §9: patch frame labeller tool, force calibration, multi-object scenes, patch-id reassignment. Next: feed these into task_hoi_isaac_retargeting_verifier_v0.

tasks.title: 做 Isaac retargeting verifier v0
tasks.description: Why now: 已经决定要用 Isaac 判断机器人是否复现同一 object-state transition。Deliverable: 一个 v0 verifier，输入 human HOI label + robot candidate trajectory，输出 object pose/contact/joint-limit/collision/failure report。Acceptance: 能在 mock scene 上跑通，并给出可解释 reject reason。

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [2026-05-23] Verifier v0 done (mock-Isaac, no physics import needed). Artifacts: isaac_verifier_v0.py (orchestrator + 9 checks), generate_mock_robot_candidates.py (good + bad case), mock_robots/good_mug_pick.json + bad_mug_pick.json, verifier-v0-spec.md (schema, check table, demo, v1 plan). Robot candidate schema 'robot_candidate.v0' covers joint_names/joint_q/joint_qdot/joint_limits/links_per_frame/predicted_object_pose/predicted_contact_intent/collision_spheres/scene_obstacles. Verify() runs 9 checks: human_label_valid, robot_candidate_valid, schema_align, joint_limits, self_collision, scene_collision, object_pose (RMSE+end pos+end rot), contact_phase_timeline (F1), sliding_consistency (cos). Demo run on samples_contact_pack/02_power_grasp_mug.json: good_mug_pick.json => exit 0, overall_pass=true, 9/9 green; bad_mug_pick.json => exit 1, 4 explicit reject reasons (joint_limits[panda_joint5 q=3.5 at frame 3 vs limit [-2.8973,2.8973]], scene_collision[tool0 vs table aabb at frame 2 distance 0.0 vs radius 0.04], object_pose[end_position_err 0.06 m > tol 0.03 m, RMSE 0.0367 m < tol 0.05 m], contact_phase_timeline[F1=0.667 < tol 0.7]). Every violation carries frame index + offending value so the retargeting solver can target the specific failure. Acceptance: runs on mock scene, gives interpretable reject reasons. v1 plan (Isaac backend, URDF-driven limits, PhysX collisions, friction-cone/penetration checks, patch-id match) listed in spec §7. Known gaps (no physics, no FK, no mass/friction) listed in §6.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [Codex route 2026-05-23] Route: start with two modalities on one identical task, not all modalities. The acceptance artifact is a common object-centric timeline: object pose/velocity, contact intent, hand/robot state, camera extrinsics, and success outcome. DexMachina and FoundationPose support functional/object-state retargeting rather than joint-angle imitation.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [2026-05-23] Smoke v0 done. Workspace: ~/workspace/diverse-same-task-dataset. Artifacts: hoi_schema.md (schema v0 contract + per-modality payload table), validate_hoi_schema.py (single-file CLI validator), generate_samples.py (emits the three sample clips), samples/sample_glove_pick_cup.json + sample_umi_pick_cup.json + sample_umi_pick_cup_BAD.json. Both valid clips share task_id='pick_red_cup_v1', object_id='red_cup_001', identical timestamps_us cadence (5 frames @ 50 ms), identical camera intrinsics/extrinsics structure, and identical canonical object endpoint (cup lifted 12 cm). Validator results: glove VALID exit=0, umi VALID exit=0, BAD INVALID exit=1 with the 4 planted reasons surfaced (missing object_id, non-monotonic timestamps, broken quat norm, broken normal norm). Acceptance: two modalities encoded under one schema, same task/object/timeline/camera fields, validator green on both. Required-field contract is documented in hoi_schema.md §3; per-modality optional payload in §4. Open issues (camera convention REP-103 vs OpenCV, pose_source enum, multi-object extension, proprio promotion) listed in §8. Hand-off to task_hoi_contact_annotation_pack: contact_intent[] is the field that grows; current spec has state enum + patch list + grasp_mode, next iteration will add per-frame phase and oriented patch frames.

Report success proxy, jerk/acceleration, event-guardrail violations, and contact-risk intervals.

tasks.title: GELLO 末端从 trigger 改 ACE-style 腕环 + MANUS 手姿态
tasks.description: DexGello input-stack update: keep GELLO responsible for Franka/GELLO arm joint-space teleop, and replace/extend the distal trigger module with an ACE-style wrist ring for wrist/intent input. Do not use ACE hand-facing camera for hand pose; hand posture, finger state, and retargeting input come from MANUS glove. Deliverable covered: GELLO A67/trigger distal interface to ACE wrist ring, keep/delete list, MANUS signal path, wrist-ring output fields, cable/quick-release/limit plan, CAD-source artifact, and fit-check decision.
tasks.result: User confirmed on 2026-06-11 that the GELLO distal trigger -> ACE-style wrist ring + MANUS hand-pose path belongs to DexGello and can be marked done.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body:
Design clarification after checking the references:
- GELLO side: keep the Franka FR3 GELLO arm chain and joint-space teleop logic. The relevant mechanical interface is the distal A67/trigger area; the Franka FR3 mechanical folder includes `20_TRIGGER_BODY.STL`, `21_TRIGGER_FINGER.STL`, and trigger storage parts, so this is the region to replace or adapt.
- ACE side: borrow only the WRIST / wearable exoskeleton geometry concept. Do not use ACE's hand-facing camera path, because MANUS glove provides the hand pose and finger state.
- Proposed merge: make an adapter that bolts onto the GELLO distal trigger mount / A67 connector and carries an ACE-style wrist ring or cuff. The ring should provide comfortable wrist anchoring / intent input and cable routing, while MANUS streams hand posture into the retargeting stack.
- First CAD target: a non-electrical fit-check print with wrist-ring size, quick-release/strap, cable exit, and hard-stop clearance; after fit passes, add any buttons/sensors only if needed.

tasks.title: 改造 10T 硬盘为组内共享 NAS
tasks.description: Track this under Real-Robot Lab Infra shared storage. Decision: buy the Taobao 7-day no-reason-return item "企业团队云硬盘盒NAS局域网共享权限自动备份远程访问WIFI" (short link: https://e.tb.cn/h.R4yF4iTokRMx0ei?tk=YJOJ5Dl6aXt, code HU006) and use the existing 10T hard drive as a group shared-file NAS for lab computers. Acceptance: confirm the purchased NAS enclosure supports the actual 10T HDD form factor/interface and power needs; install the disk; configure LAN/Wi-Fi access, shared folders, user/group permissions, automatic backup, and remote-access policy; record stable hostname/IP, SMB/NFS/WebDAV or vendor client path, filesystem/volume state, simple read/write throughput from at least one group computer, and any 7-day return decision if compatibility fails.

tasks.description: 当前数据已全部 replay 至少一遍；十几条 gripper-tip 交互方式的数据已剔除，保留 90 条 gripper-face 交互数据。四个方案正在训练: (1) 75 train / 10 validation / 5 test 原始手部视觉；(2) 同样 split，但把手部区域替换成灰色背景；(3) 90 条全部训练，原始手部视觉；(4) 90 条全部训练，手部区域替换成灰色背景。Acceptance: 收集四个 run 的 checkpoint、训练/validation 曲线、test 或真机部署结果；明确 Plan 1 vs Plan 2 的手部视觉影响，Plan 1 vs Plan 3 的数据量影响，并决定是否追加原始数据 + 干净 replay 数据的混合配比实验。

comments.author: Boris
comments.body: Update from Boris: all data has been replayed at least once. A dozen-plus episodes were removed because they used a different interaction style, contacting with the gripper tip instead of the gripper face. Current clean dataset has 90 episodes. Four training schemes are running: Plan 1 = 75 train / 10 validation / 5 test; Plan 2 = same split but hand region replaced by gray background; Plan 3 = all 90 for training; Plan 4 = all 90 for training with hand region replaced by gray background. Plan 1 vs Plan 2 measures whether hand visual representation affects real-robot deployment interaction. Plan 1 vs Plan 3 measures whether more data helps. After these four results, consider mixing original raw data and clean replay data.

tasks.title: Real-Robot Demo: Jenga 抽积木塔 tactile pilot
tasks.description: 把 Jenga-style block extraction / 抽积木塔作为紧凑真机 demo：机器人从木块塔里推出或抽出指定木块，同时尽量不让塔倒。前置采购 / 确认：Jenga 积木塔或自定义积木块堆、目标块标记材料（贴纸 / 彩色胶带）、防滑底座或防滑垫。用 q_arm、T_hand、q_finger 记录链路和 tactile/VTA/VLA 分析接触稳定、摩擦/滑移、侧向推力、塔体晃动和失败恢复；配一个可打印/定制的特殊 gripper，并接入戴盟触觉。目标是把 slip、contact stability、lateral force、tower wobble、tower collapse 等失败变成真机 demo 和评测样例。DexGello 可提供数据/控制依赖，但 demo 场景归 Real-Robot Demos 表格管理。
tasks.assignee: master student TBD / Zheng Yuhang for gripper timing

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Scope note from Boris: Jenga-style block extraction / 抽积木塔 can be a compact training/evaluation task for a master student. Use Daimon tactile sensing and a special gripper; ask Zheng Yuhang whether the Daimon-side gripper package matched to the previous UMI gripper is ready and when it can be brought to Singapore.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Naming correction: this is not generic stacking. The intended object is Jenga-style block extraction / 抽积木塔: push or pull one wooden block out of a tower while preserving tower stability. The useful tactile signals are slip, lateral force, contact stability, tower wobble, and collapse risk.

tasks.title: 更新 Cosmos Predict 2.5 双腕拼接 system prompt
tasks.description: Rewrite the Cosmos Predict 2.5 system prompt for UMI concatenated wrist views. It must say: the image/video is a horizontal concatenation; left half is the left wrist/gripper view; right half is the right wrist/gripper view; lower visible gripper parts are the interactive end-effectors; keep left/right identity, object state, contact geometry, and gripper-object interaction consistent. Acceptance: save the prompt template and run or dry-run it on representative clips.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Prompt wording should prevent Cosmos from treating the concatenated frame as a generic collage. It needs explicit semantics for left wrist, right wrist, and visible lower gripper parts as interactive robot end-effectors.

tasks.title: 调研 UMI action 注入路径
tasks.description: Compare practical ways to inject robot action into the UMI world model: (1) text/action tokens in prompt, (2) first-frame plus structured caption, (3) trajectory/control maps rendered into image/video conditions, (4) extra latent/input channels for pose/gripper maps, (5) cross-attention or adapter tokens, (6) AdaLN/time-step modulation with action embeddings, (7) Ctrl-World-style action-conditioned UNet/action encoder, and (8) discrete action tokenization. Acceptance: produce a short decision table with implementation cost, expected action faithfulness, dual-arm support, compatibility with WAN2.2 TI2V, and recommended v1 path.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Initial framing: WAN2.2 TI2V supports text+image conditioning, but UMI needs action faithfulness beyond first-frame appearance. Use Ctrl-World/action-conditioned video models and LingBot-style active world simulation as references, then choose the smallest WAN-compatible v1 injection method.

tasks.title: 用 AffinityOnLinux / GIMP 替代 Photoshop 分图层流程
tasks.description: Ubuntu 没有 Photoshop 时，把 agentic prompt 生成的固定位置分元素图片接到 Linux 可运行的图层编辑流程。优先评估两条路线：(1) seapear/AffinityOnLinux: 通过 Wine 在 GNU/Linux 上运行 Affinity Photo/Designer/Publisher，用来复现 PSD/图层检查体验；(2) GIMP: 原生免费开源图像编辑器，作为可脚本化 baseline。Acceptance: 记录 AffinityOnLinux 安装/启动可行性或 blocker；写出 GIMP 批处理或 Script-Fu/Python-Fu 路线；对 poster 和 robot_still 各跑一次白底去除、透明图层叠加、原始相对位置保持、layer naming、导出 .xcf/.afphoto 或 PSD/PNG round-trip；输出 reconstruction diff、缺失/重复元素检查、命令/log/sha256 和最终推荐的 Ubuntu 默认路线。

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Reference checked 2026-05-27: https://github.com/seapear/AffinityOnLinux is a community guide for running Affinity software on GNU/Linux with Wine, recommending routes such as Wine 10.17+, Lutris, Heroic, Bottles, or Rum and warning that it is not affiliated with Serif/Canva. https://www.gimp.org/ describes GIMP as a cross-platform free/open-source image editor available on GNU/Linux, macOS, Windows, with scripting/plugin extensibility. Use AffinityOnLinux for a closer Photoshop-like editor if feasible, and GIMP as the native reproducible fallback.

comments.author: Host verification
comments.body: 本机 Codex 在 feibo@100.87.220.29 的 `~/workspace/image-layered-world-model` 完成 Ubuntu 分图层替代流程。`linux_layer_editor_pipeline.py` sha256 `3f1e3e3a8d6572e5c58d6be89c2579a6f7f33bc1f1d91e31f410f66fbd956af2`，运行 `.venv/bin/python linux_layer_editor_pipeline.py --cases poster robot_still` 退出 0。Report `/home/feibo/workspace/image-layered-world-model/outputs_linux_layer_editor/linux_layer_editor_report.md` sha256 `ce8f6dd98876d7ae36d1e885542bdd8661eaeb85da12b8e2dea605b2ff3c2c4b`; summary `/home/feibo/workspace/image-layered-world-model/outputs_linux_layer_editor/summary.json` sha256 `901485a8a4783472240fc96a26a114cb3415ae4e97fc4434d3a36ddf91c397db`。结果: poster recon_l1_rgb=0.040050、无 duplicate pairs；robot_still recon_l1_rgb=0.010391、duplicate pair 0_1 被标出；两个 case 均导出命名透明 PNG、diff map 和 raw PSD roundtrip。环境结论: AffinityOnLinux blocked because Wine/Affinity missing；GIMP binary missing，所以生成了 GIMP-ready batch script 但未导出 XCF。推荐 Ubuntu 默认路线为 Python/Pillow+pytoshop 的 CI-verifiable PNG/PSD roundtrip，GIMP 仅作为安装后人工 XCF 检查。

comments.body: Strict validation 2026-05-29: existing report shows pytoshop PSD route works, but GIMP/Wine/Affinity are missing on yuhang-B850M-C. sudo requires a password, so GIMP cannot be installed non-interactively; keep blocked until GIMP/Affinity is installed or sudo is provided.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: ICRA 2026 poster reference from user: Foundational World Models Accurately Detect Bimanual Manipulator Failures (arXiv:2603.06987), Stanford / Watney / TRI. Key relevance: the first assisted-teleop loop can be a runtime failure monitor rather than a full future-video preview. The paper trains a probabilistic, history-informed world model in NVIDIA Cosmos Tokenizer latent space using nominal bimanual manipulation trajectories; uses uncertainty and prediction error as nonconformity scores; calibrates thresholds with conformal prediction without failure data; and detects real bimanual cable-drop failures. Reported evidence: Bimanual Cable Manipulation dataset with synchronized camera views plus proprio/action, ~569.7k trainable parameters, ~92% weighted classification accuracy on the real dataset, and real-time inference around 11 Hz on H100 in the poster. Recommendation: update the matrix to add a Cosmos-latent failure-monitor route and treat it as the first operator-facing on/off experiment for warning lead time, false alarm rate, and correction success.

tasks.title: Prototype Cosmos-latent teleop failure monitor
tasks.description: Use the ICRA 2026 Foundational World Models failure-detection paper as the first concrete Model-Assisted Teleoperation loop. Build a minimal offline prototype that takes nominal teleop episodes, encodes multi-view video plus robot state/action history, trains a compact probabilistic world model or a documented Cosmos-tokenizer fallback, calibrates a conformal threshold on nominal validation clips, and overlays an uncertainty / nonconformity warning on held-out normal and induced-failure clips. Acceptance: source paper notes; exact data schema; at least one recorded or synthetic contact-rich task candidate such as cable/plug, slime-meat cutting, insertion, or handoff; command log; plots of uncertainty/error over time; false-positive estimate on nominal clips; warning lead-time estimate on failure/near-failure clips; and a dashboard recommendation on whether to promote this survey card into an engineering prototype.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Archive decision from user-provided reference: HandelBot: Real-World Piano Playing via Fast Adaptation of Dexterous Robot Policies (arXiv:2603.12243) covers the actionable embodied-music target. It uses a sim-trained bimanual dexterous policy, structured real-world refinement for key/finger alignment, residual RL with MIDI output as reward, and real hardware experiments across five songs. Keep HandelBot as the reference; reopen only for a distinct non-piano contact-to-sound dataset or instrument task tied to lab hardware.

tasks.title: Score open-hardware collection platforms
tasks.description: Compare OpenArm, AmazingHand, mobile dual-arm base concepts, dense dexterous hands, and low-cost humanoid/mobile platforms. Acceptance: decision table with cost, build risk, sensing, calibration, safety, and compatibility with UMI/HOI/VTLA schemas.
tasks.result: Archived as inactive Survey TODO on 2026-06-12. This remains background/scoping material, but it is no longer an active dashboard TODO.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: References checked: OpenArm, AmazingHand, AhaRobot, Berkeley Humanoid Lite, and adjacent open-hand/humanoid hardware references. Build/no-build should be based on data value per setup hour.

tasks.title: Choose first assembly benchmark scope
tasks.description: Decide whether fractured-object reassembly or soft-wrist insertion is the lower-risk first benchmark. Acceptance: state chosen benchmark, required assets, physical setup, policy inputs, simulator/real split, and success/failure metrics.
tasks.result: Archived the combined Fracture / Soft-Wrist Assembly survey card. ShapeForce covers the low-cost soft-wrist contact-rich manipulation direction directly; fractured-object reassembly should only reopen as a separate, narrower benchmark with concrete physical assets and owner.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Primary references checked: Breaking Bad Dataset and Robotic Object Insertion with a Soft Wrist through Sim-to-Real Privileged Training. Survey should test whether they share one benchmark or should split later.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Archive decision from user-provided paper: ShapeForce: Low-Cost Soft Robotic Wrist for Contact-Rich Manipulation (arXiv:2511.19955) directly covers the standalone soft-wrist/contact-feedback route with a low-cost plug-and-play compliant wrist, marker-tracked deformation, force-like signals, and contact-rich manipulation evaluation comparable to six-axis force-torque sensing. Keep ShapeForce as a reference, not a separate active Survey project. The fracture-reassembly half is not solved by ShapeForce, but it should reopen only as an independent benchmark with real objects, simulator/real split, metrics, and owner.

tasks.title: 制定 fixed-exo + dual-wrist 数据采集 / 标注 schema
tasks.description: Define the synchronized episode schema for the new project under the updated data reality: existing UMI gives left wrist fisheye, right wrist fisheye, and D435 main view, but no fixed third-person/exo view. The schema must support both current data and future/new fixed-exo capture: timestamps, camera intrinsics/extrinsics where available, dual-arm EEF pose, gripper state, task caption, object IDs, train/val/test split, optional fixed-exo_absent/fixed-exo_synthesized flags, and optional layer pseudo-label paths. Acceptance: JSON schema or manifest example for one current-data episode plus one future tri-view episode; explicit checklist for what old UMI data lacks and what new capture must add.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Data update from project discussion: old Yuhang/Daimon UMI data has no fixed third-person/exo view. The schema must represent the available two wrist fisheye + D435 main-view format and mark fixed-exo as absent, synthesized, or newly captured.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Dataset-selection update: use MolmoAct's original side/external + single-wrist real-robot data and MolmoAct2-BimanualYAM's LeRobot top/left/right + 14D bimanual state/action schema as references for packaging and ablation design. Do not assume they solve our data gap: current UMI/Jianzhi data still lacks the third robot-ego fixed view, so the manifest must explicitly encode camera provenance and fixed_view_status = absent/synthesized/new_capture.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Remote schema/audit pass is ready for review at jingxiang@100.112.245.1:/home/jingxiang/workspace/das-data/codebases/tri_view_layered_wm. Added tri-view manifest schema plus two examples: current UMI/DAS-style dual-wrist + D435/main evidence with fixed_view_status=absent, and future tri-view capture with left_wrist + right_wrist + front_ego.fixed_mount=true. Validator passed on the target machine. Data audit conclusion: local DAS / 10Kh RealOmni and current UMI/Jianzhi packs are partial only; they have robot0/robot1 or left/right wrist evidence but no real synchronized fixed front/top stream. `test_sets/ctrlworld/views/wrist.mp4` is explicitly a crop placeholder, not a third camera. Closest public full-match candidates are ALOHA/LeRobot datasets exposing cam_high + cam_left_wrist + cam_right_wrist with 14D state/action; MolmoAct2-BimanualYAM is a strong bimanual schema/model reference but needs camera-mount verification because public examples are top/left/right, not explicitly left/right wrist. DROID is not a full match because it is single-arm.

tasks.title: Add Video2Sim2Real-style anchor contract to Real2Sim2Real
tasks.description: 把 Video2Sim2Real (arXiv:2606.08828) 的单视频 Real2Sim2Real 机制整理成 AgenticSim 可执行 contract。核心不是直接全轨迹 retarget，而是从人类视频构建 simulator-ready digital twin 和 object/robot motion priors，抽 object-centric keyframes，优化 robot configuration anchors，再用 IL 做 real point-cloud recalibration、residual RL 做局部 finger/contact adaptation，并保留 collision-aware planning / verifier。Acceptance: 1) 写出 manifest schema：video source、digital twin assets、object tracks、object-centric keyframes、robot anchor configs、recalibration samples、residual adaptation hooks、planner/verifier outputs；2) 选一个 tabletop manipulation task 做最小样例；3) 说明哪些模块可先用现有 AgenticSim mock/Isaac/Genesis/LeHome 接口跑，哪些依赖真实视频/点云/hand hardware；4) 给出接入 R2S2RPipeline、TraceLoader、MemoryStore 和 GovernanceGate 的字段映射；5) 输出 RESULT.md 或 dashboard comment，明确是否下一步做 runnable implementation。

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Reference added from user: Video2Sim2Real (https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08828, project page https://video2sim2real.github.io/). Key update for our Real2Sim2Real section: treat human video as a source for digital twin + object-centric anchors, not as a reliable full robot-motion target. Candidate AgenticSim modules: perception/digital-twin import, object-keyframe anchor optimizer, IL recalibration from noisy point clouds, residual RL contact adaptation, and collision-aware planning/verifier for spatial generalization.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body:
Moved from Self-Improving intro to corresponding TODO during 2026-06-24 intro cleanup:
Real2Sim2Real reference update 2026-06-09: Video2Sim2Real should inform the concrete single-video path. For AgenticSim, split the contract into: 1) reconstruct a simulator-ready digital twin and object/robot motion priors from a human video; 2) detect object-centric keyframes instead of trusting full human-retargeted motion; 3) optimize robot configuration anchors inside the simulator; 4) learn real-world point-cloud recalibration via IL; 5) use residual RL for local finger/contact adaptation; 6) keep collision-aware planning for spatial generalization.

tasks.title: Cosmos3 测试
tasks.description: 测试 nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID 等 Cosmos / Cosmos3 相关视频生成模型能否作为 action-conditioned Ego World baseline。重点包括：实时外部 action condition、双腕和机器人头部 ego views 的共享 context、纯视频生成模型对去掉前景 robot layer / 保留 object-interaction-scene 的 prompt 响应，以及与 Gamma-World / Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers / Cosmos2.5 的最小对照。Acceptance: 至少一组可复现输入、命令或调用记录、输出样例、失败模式记录，并给出是否值得进入 Ego World + IDM 训练链路。
tasks.result: Completed 2026-06-16. nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID and Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers (5B) were both run end-to-end on held-out UMI-style clips with reproducible prompts, logs, and galleries. Result: Cosmos3 prompt-long rerun changes outputs only slightly; Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers adds motion but is still unstable on dual-view consistency and object fidelity. Recommendation: treat this baseline-comparison task as closed, and do not promote either current checkpoint directly into the Ego World + IDM training chain without stronger UMI-specific fine-tuning or a better Stage-1 baseline.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Done and verified on 2026-06-16. Ran nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID end-to-end on the H20 server with held-out UMI-style samples, including an old generic-prompt run and a prompt_long-derived rerun; then downloaded the outputs into /Users/boris/Downloads/cosmos3_umi_worldmodel_20260616 with logs, sample videos, frame-review grids, and a report page at /Users/boris/Downloads/cosmos3_umi_worldmodel_20260616/index.html. Also ran Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers (5B; local artifact path: /root/autodl-tmp/models/Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Turbo-Diffusers) as the minimal comparator on the same 4 samples at 1280x704, 49 frames, 4 steps, with logs under logs/wan22_turbo_fullres_4x.log and summary under logs/wan22_turbo_fullres_summary.json. Main finding: prompt improvement alone does not rescue nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID; Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers produces stronger motion but still has heavy artifacts and weak dual-view consistency. This closes the baseline-testing TODO, but the next-stage open work remains tri-view dataset replacement, Gamma-World reproduction/build-upon, and layered dataset preparation before deciding a production Stage-1 backbone.

tasks.title: 实现 Wan2.1 双腕拼接 baseline 与 dual-latent 结构草案
tasks.description: 根据 2026-06-17 UMI Model Structure 更新，先用 Wan2.1 而不是 Wan2.2 做 Route A/B：Route A 为左右 wrist-camera 画面拼接后送入 Wan2.1 finetune，数据源先检查 10Kh RealOmni-Open Dataset，完成 text annotation 与 video stitching recipe；Route B 为 shared-backbone dual-branch Wan2.1 训练草案：left/right video latent、view embedding、robot proprio/state、future action、camera pose/calibration、task text tokens、cross-view fusion、shared Wan2.1 backbone、dual prediction heads / shared VAE decoder。Wan2.2-A14B 只作为后续对照，因为其 high-noise / low-noise MoE 需要分专家训练和组合验证。AutoDL 训练约束：按单机最多 8 卡设计，不依赖跨机分布式训练。Acceptance: 数据可用性与字段审计、拼接 baseline manifest、dual-latent token schema、Wan2.1 单机 8-GPU 内可跑的训练配置/伪代码、显存/批量/梯度累积或 checkpointing 方案、Wan2.2 high/low 复杂度说明、与 Gamma-World baseline 和后续 Image-Layered/SVG track 的边界说明。
tasks.result: Closed on 2026-06-22 per Boris: UMI should keep only three open TODOs now (VACE video inpainting, global layered dataset plan, and FoundationPose layer utility). This item is no longer an active UMI TODO; preserve its prior notes as background/context.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Seeded from user-provided UMI Model Structure screenshot on 2026-06-17. Key points: (1) direct stitching + Wan2.1 finetune over left/right wrist-camera frames; data source candidate 10Kh RealOmni-Open Dataset; prep includes text annotation and video stitching. (2) shared-backbone dual-branch Wan: left wrist-camera -> left video latent, right wrist-camera -> right video latent, add view embeddings to latent tokens, train shared Wan2.1 backbone with dual outputs.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Research update 2026-06-17: prefer Wan2.1 for first UMI dual-wrist training. Official Wan2.2 docs/model cards describe A14B as a two-expert MoE denoiser: high-noise expert handles early/high-noise/layout steps, low-noise expert handles later/detail steps, with an SNR/timestep switch. Community training reports in DiffSynth-Studio also show practical instability when separately fine-tuning high vs low noise experts; one report says the same task learned better on Wan2.1 than Wan2.2. Wan2.1 has simpler single-backbone training and mature LoRA/full-finetune recipes in VideoX-Fun, finetrainers, DiffSynth-Studio, and diffusion-pipe. Decision: implement Route A concat baseline and Route B dual-latent prototype on Wan2.1 first; keep Wan2.2-A14B for later quality comparison after the schema works, or evaluate Wan2.2-TI2V-5B only if we specifically need its dense 5B unified TI2V path.

comments.author: Boris / Codex
comments.body: Compute constraint note 2026-06-17: AutoDL cannot be used for cross-machine distributed training in this plan, so UMI Wan2.1 training should assume at most one 8-GPU machine. Do not design the first route around multi-node DeepSpeed/FSDP. Keep the initial training recipe inside single-node 8-card capacity with LoRA first, then optional small full-finetune, gradient accumulation, checkpointing/offload, and reduced resolution/frames if needed. This makes Wan2.1 even more attractive than Wan2.2-A14B high/low expert finetuning for v0.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Remote execution on /root/autodl-tmp/workspace/DiffSynth-Studio completed a Wan2.2-A14B paired output-residual + action/state consistency auxiliary-head grid. All 8 high/low step-64 checkpoints were produced for ac weights 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5. Strict action gate stamp 20260621_201427 evaluated rows 0,1,5,7,11 against zero, time_view_only, and wrong-row controls. Result: 2/20 pass overall: ac0.05 0/5, ac0.1 1/5, ac0.2 1/5, ac0.5 0/5. This is negative evidence: the current auxiliary-head route is trainable but not reliably action-conditioned. Local report: /Users/boris/Downloads/umi_paired_actcons_gate_20260621/index.html.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Raw sensitivity probe added on the remote DiffSynth workspace: scripts_umi/probe_paired_condition_sensitivity.py. For paired_outres_actcons ac0.2 step-64 row0, actual-vs-zero/time_view_only/wrongrow noise prediction deltas were tiny in the high expert (~0.006 MAE, 0.58-0.68% relative) and larger but still insufficient in the low expert (~0.081-0.096 MAE, 6.6-7.8% relative). Interpretation: existing output-residual + auxiliary action/state head mostly leaves the high-noise stage uncontrolled, so video rollout can drift before low expert correction. Prepared next route: scripts_umi/launch_a14b_paired_xctx_control_grid8.sh, which enables UMI_BLOCK_CONTROL_USE_X_CONTEXT=1 plus paired dual branch and output residual. Launch is waiting because the 8 H20 GPUs are currently occupied by Gamma-World/Cosmos2.5 training.

tasks.title: 收敛分层方案并启动 global layered dataset 计划
tasks.description: 当前收敛结论：采用 iterative segment + inpaint 的 v0 global layered dataset 路线，而不是直接依赖端到端 layer decomposition。处理顺序先 robot-first，再 object/contact/occluder-next，最后 scene/background-final：先用 SAM3/SAM2 或 mask tracking 分割 robot layer，生成 robot mask/rgba/video/SVG sidecar；再用 FLUX.fill、VACE、LaMa 或同类 inpaint 模型把 robot 从原视频中抹掉并补全被遮挡区域。随后按 task-relevant object/contact/occluder 逐个 object layer 迭代：每次 segment 一个对象/接触区域，导出该层 mask/rgba/video/SVG sidecar，再对剩余视频做 inpaint，直到得到 scene/background layer。背景层优先面向第三人称或固定机位视频，预期随时间变化很小；wrist/ego view 背景变化较大，需要单独标记 temporal consistency 风险。2026-06-24 ClawCross 同步 Layered Image Policy 最新进度：SAM-HQ robot mask review 结果不如 SAM3，SAM-HQ 暂时排除；SAM3/SAM2 对 gripper mask 仍有问题，下一步测试 prompt `robot arm with black gripper` 与更低 probability-map threshold；LaMa 结果未出；RoboTwin2.0 dataset (https://huggingface.co/datasets/TianxingChen/RoboTwin2.0/tree/main/dataset) 可用于仿真 GT 分图层验证。候选方法仍作为组件或对照保留：SAM3+GPT inpaint、VACE/VAC/VAS video inpaint、FLUX.fill/LaMa still or video-adapted inpaint、RevealLayer/Qwen 负样例参考、RoboTwin synthetic masks、MaskWAM-style VLM->SAM3->human-verification mask propagation、SuperSVG/LIVE/SAM-contour SVG sidecar，StarVector/OmniSVG 作为可选 SVG probe。参考 MaskWAM 数据处理管线：language annotation / VLM 提取 task-relevant objects，用 SAM3 text/point condition 传播 mask sequence，多轮 human verification 只修正失败/歧义 round，最后写入 labeled episodes；UMI 需要把这个单 mask pipeline 扩展成 robot、object/contact、occluder、scene/background 的多层 pseudo-label，并保留 mask.png / rgba.png / video / inpainted_background.png / SVG sidecar / layer_manifest.json。Acceptance: iterative pipeline 的方法矩阵、每轮输入/输出 schema、速度/成本、人为标注需求、temporal consistency、recomposition QA、FoundationPose/IDM/reward utility、失败样例；给出 v0 dataset 构建决策、robot-first/object-next/background-final 的处理顺序、下一批 episode/image/video 清单。

tasks.title: 验证 Stage 3 layer utility：scene geometry、object HOI/pose/flow、robot pose/retargeting
tasks.description:
Ziyang/Boris 用分层结果验证三类下游 utility，不再只看 FoundationPose。

Scene Layer: 用 RoboEngine (https://roboengine.github.io) 做物理/任务感知背景替换与 data augmentation；补 MoGe (https://github.com/microsoft/moge) 估计 depth / point map / normal / camera FOV，GeoCalib (https://github.com/cvg/GeoCalib) 估计 intrinsics / gravity direction，用于把 object flow/pose lift 到稳定场景坐标。

Object / Contact Layer: 2D 分割/掩码追踪用 SAM3 (https://github.com/facebookresearch/sam3)；3D 形状和 6-DoF/pose 用 SAM 3D Objects (https://github.com/facebookresearch/sam-3d-objects)、Fast-SAM3D (https://github.com/wlfeng0509/Fast-SAM3D)、FoundationPose、Fast-FoundationStereoPhysics、Orient-Anything、Any6D；物体运动/形变辅助估计用 BootsTAPIR/TAPNet (https://github.com/google-deepmind/tapnet)、AllTracker、D4RT、CoTracker、Any4D，并在需要时结合 Video Depth Anything / MoGe 做 3D point flow。Do-As-I-Do (https://do-as-i-do.com/ ; https://github.com/malik-group/do-as-i-do) 作为 hand-object reconstruction + retargeting bridge：评价 object pose/velocity trajectory、contact intent、grasp/tool-use mode、task result 是否一致，而不是只比关节角。

Robot / Hand Layer: 可见机器人用 Dr. Robot 做 image-based robot pose；腕部/头部 ego-view 用 ORB-SLAM3 / VGGT-SLAM 做 VO/SLAM；人手源视频用 HaWoR (https://hawor-project.github.io/ ; https://github.com/ThunderVVV/HaWoR) 做 world-space hand tracking，再用 Do-As-I-Do / dex-retargeting 连接到 robot layer / IDM。

Acceptance: 选 3-5 个对象/片段，输出 raw RGB、mask-only、object-only、object+background、robot-only、recomposed clip 的 pose/flow/retarget/eval 结果；每层报告成功率或人工评分、遮挡/背景干扰案例、几何/标定误差、contact/flow 稳定性、是否 object/contact layer 比 flat RGB 或 mask-only 更有用；明确哪些工具进入 v0 pipeline，哪些只作为对照或失败样例。

Robot/hand adds HaWoR and Do-As-I-Do/dex-retargeting next to Dr. Robot and wrist-view VO/SLAM. Do-As-I-Do should be evaluated as matched object/contact/task outcome retargeting, not joint-angle imitation.
